```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
E     <- sample(c(0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0), 1)
sigma <- sample(c(1, 2, 3, 4), 1)

## Respostas
correct <- ceiling((1.96 * sigma / E)^2)
w1 <- ceiling(1.96 * sigma / E)
w2 <- ceiling((1.645 * sigma / E)^2)
w3 <- ceiling((1.96 * E / sigma)^2)
w4 <- floor((1.96 * sigma / E)^2)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para estimar $\mu$ com margem de erro maxima $E = `r E`$ e confianca 95% ($z_{0{,}975} = 1{,}96$), com desvio padrao $\sigma = `r sigma`$ conhecido, o tamanho amostral minimo necessario e $n \geq \left(\frac{1{,}96\,\sigma}{E}\right)^2$. O valor de $n$ e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
$n \geq \left(\frac{1{,}96 \times `r sigma`}{`r E`}\right)^2 = `r (1.96*sigma/E)^2`$, logo $n = `r correct`$ (teto da expressao).

Erros comuns: nao elevar ao quadrado; usar $z_{0{,}95}=1{,}645$; inverter $E$ e $\sigma$; ou usar o piso em vez do teto.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap5S1Q3_tamanho_amostral
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
